text.title: error-handling Failure Modes
text.summary: Six antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Interpolating exception messages, stack traces, SQL text, or internal paths into the detail field for 5xx errors.
antipattern.symptom: Production error bodies contain `OperationalError`, `Traceback`, `psycopg2`, or hostnames; security scanner flags PII/credentials leak.
antipattern.root-cause: Default exception handlers render exc.__str__ into the response without sanitization.
antipattern.fix: Catch broadly in the global handler; log exc_info=True server-side; return a static detail string and the traceId for client-side reference.

antipattern.description: Returning Content-Type: application/json instead of application/problem+json.
antipattern.symptom: Spring RestClient.ProblemDetail / RFC 7807-aware clients fall back to generic parsing; type-based dispatch dies silently.
antipattern.root-cause: Framework default JSONResponse sets `application/json`; methodology requires explicit override.
antipattern.fix: Set `headers={"Content-Type": "application/problem+json"}` on every error response, or override the framework's default media type globally.

antipattern.description: Body status field disagrees with HTTP response code.
antipattern.symptom: HTTP 502 but body says `"status": 400`; clients route to the wrong handler.
antipattern.root-cause: Downstream errors get re-wrapped without updating the body status; or middleware overrides HTTP code after handler returns body.
antipattern.fix: Build ProblemDetail with `status=response.status_code`; assert in the contract test that the two match.

antipattern.symptom: Clients receive an empty errors[] on 404 or a single fabricated entry on 500; confused parsing.
antipattern.root-cause: Misreading errors[] as a general-purpose error list rather than a validation-failures extension.
antipattern.fix: Restrict errors[] to 4xx validation responses (typically 400, 422). For other 4xx/5xx, omit the field entirely.

antipattern.description: Per-route error responses with custom shapes ({"error":...}, {"message":...}, HTML).
antipattern.symptom: Same API returns three different error envelopes depending on the endpoint; client-side error-handling code multiplies.
antipattern.root-cause: Multiple developers added per-route exception handlers instead of one framework-level handler.
antipattern.fix: Remove per-route handlers. Register one global handler returning ProblemDetail. CI grep blocks `JSONResponse(content={"error":` patterns.

antipattern.description: Missing traceId on every error response.
antipattern.symptom: On-call cannot correlate a customer-supplied error report with any log line; resorts to timestamp scraping across services.
antipattern.root-cause: traceparent header not propagated; no fallback UUID generation.
antipattern.fix: In the global handler, read `request.headers["traceparent"]`; if absent, generate `uuid.uuid4().hex` and set on the response.
